title: Review: fix: address P0-P3 review findings across all backends
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Four P1 risks: (1) name param fossilized as ignored in public API before 1.0; (2) bitwarden auth cached permanently with no re-auth on expiry; (3) with_max_entries(0) is a public OOM footgun. All are API design issues that need resolution before a v1.0 release. No security vulnerabilities in the fixes themselves.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) json_skip_container cross-type nesting with pathological malformed JSON inputs — I traced several cases and believe it is correct for valid JSON but did not exhaustively prove it; (2) bitwarden_sdk Client lifetime semantics (whether reusing client across org_id-changing scenarios is safe); (3) azure-kv ChainedCredential behavior on partial failure not tested. Would raise confidence if: extract_field had tests for \uXXXX escapes (especially surrogate pairs), a fuzzer run against json_extract_string_field, and integration test coverage of bitwarden OnceCell behavior with a real token rotation.
